task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser open to the Webmin interface, typically showing the Webmin logo, left-hand navigation menu, or system dashboard.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the IP Access Control settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'IP Access Control' settings page being visible, usually accessed via Webmin -> Webmin Configuration.

task_completion.description: Select the option to restrict access to listed addresses
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a selected radio button or setting that explicitly restricts access, such as 'Only allow from listed addresses'.

task_completion.description: Enter the localhost IP address into the allowed list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact IP address '127.0.0.1' typed into the text box for allowed addresses.

task_completion.description: Enter the Admin VPN IP address into the allowed list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact IP address '10.8.0.42' typed into the text box for allowed addresses alongside the localhost IP.

task_completion.description: Save the IP Access Control configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the cursor clicking the 'Save' button on the IP Access Control page, or a subsequent confirmation message indicating the settings were successfully applied.

integrity.description: Agent used the Webmin web interface rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the agent used the Webmin web GUI in a browser to make changes, rather than opening a terminal and editing /etc/webmin/miniserv.conf directly with a text editor like nano or vim.

integrity.description: No additional or wildcard IPs were added to the allowed list
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the allowed addresses text box to ensure ONLY '127.0.0.1' and '10.8.0.42' are present. No other IPs, subnets, or wildcards should be listed.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine Webmin application
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the screenshots show a real web browser with a URL bar and authentic Webmin UI elements, not a static text file or simulated interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring Webmin's IP access control. The underlying configuration file modified by this action is /etc/webmin/miniserv.conf. The required allowed IPs are exactly ["127.0.0.1", "10.8.0.42"]. The VLM should verify these exact IPs are entered in the Webmin GUI and that access is restricted to only these addresses.
